title: Lesson 32 — Hidden Meanings

words.definition: to use ambiguous language to avoid committing to a clear position
words.example: Pressed for a yes or no, the minister only equivocated.

words.definition: to deliberately make something unclear or hard to understand
words.example: Dense legal jargon obfuscated the contract's real terms.

words.definition: a mild or indirect word substituted for one considered harsh or blunt
words.example: "Letting people go" is a euphemism for firing them.

words.definition: to suggest a meaning beyond the literal one
words.example: "Home" connotes warmth in a way "house" does not.

words.definition: to be a sign of; to indicate the literal meaning of
words.example: A red flag on the beach denotes dangerous surf.

words.definition: to suggest or refer to indirectly
words.example: The speech alluded to troubles without naming them.

words.definition: to suggest something negative in an indirect and unpleasant way
words.example: He insinuated that the results had been faked.

words.definition: to state or make known indirectly
words.example: She intimated that changes were coming without saying what.
There was an implicit warning in his calm tone.

words.definition: stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion
words.example: The instructions were explicit about the deadline.
A veiled threat lay beneath his polite words.

words.definition: not expressed or done in a direct way; indirect
words.example: He made only an oblique reference to the layoffs.

story: The memo was a masterpiece of saying nothing. Wherever a plain answer was needed, the CEO seemed to [[equivocate|equivocate]], and whole paragraphs appeared designed to [[obfuscate|obfuscate]] rather than explain. "Restructuring" was the [[euphemism|euphemism]] of the month; the word did not [[denote|denote]] layoffs directly, though everyone knew what it [[connote|connoted]]. The memo would [[allude|allude]] to "difficult choices" and seemed to [[insinuate|insinuate]] that some departments were safer than others, without ever [[intimate|intimating]] which. Nothing was [[explicit|explicit]]; every warning stayed [[implicit|implicit]], every threat [[veiled|veiled]], every reference [[oblique|oblique]] enough to deny later.
